reviews.review_description: Unfortunately not smooth motion and inconsistent. Second room, my walking suddenly sped up and just stayed that way, opened drawers and could just never let go to move on.  etc.  bought several months ago. Gave up, giving it another try now. No improvement.

developer_response.body: Hello. Sorry to hear you are having problems. Please send me a message via "contact us" at forthinteract.com so I can diagnose if there is anything I can fix. About the walking fast, I suspect you switched from "walk" to "run", which is done by clicking down on the thumb-stick you use to move. Click down on it again and it will revert to walk speed. But please contact me so I can understand more about the issues you raised.

reviews.review_description: I want this to be a good game, but so far, it's just a frustrating game with horrible physics. I was trying to do the tutorial where it tells you to pick up the gear and place it on the highlighted spindle. The highlight was on a wall with no spindle. I turned around to see if i missed something, and then the highlight jumped to the actual spindle. I literally spent 20 minutes trying to put the gear on the spindle, and I couldn't do it. My hand flopped about with the gear in hand, and even when lined up with the red gear highlight, it still wouldn't go on. It just falls through the spindle to the floor. I finally had enough and just quit the game. If I can't get past the tutorial, something is wrong.

developer_response.body:
Hello

Sorry to hear you're having problems. Thank you contacting me via the contact form at forthinteract.com as this is the best way to get support. I responded to you 33 minutes later requesting further info so I can see what's wrong. I am concerned that you said "The highlight was on a wall with no spindle" as this should never happen. I look forward to receiving your response so I can check out for any issues. Until then, the notes below might help as to what is expected behaviour. 

Gear Out is a puzzle game where you attach gears to spindles in a specific order to make them all drive each other. In the tutorial, one of the spindles lights up in blue to guide you where to attach a gear.  This only happens in the tutorial.  When you are holding a gear in your hand, you can try and place it on a spindle - either directly by moving the gear to the rough vicinity of your intended spindle, or by pointing your laser at a spindle. The spindle will light up green if it is possible for the gear to be attached or red if it is not possible. The main reason why it might not be possible to attach a gear to a spindle is that the gear is too large to fit the available space.  So if the spindle goes green and you let go of the gear, it will snap into place on the spindle. Otherwise if you let a gear go when the spindle is red (or you haven't highlighted any spindle) then the gear will just drop to the floor as any other object will if you let it go.

Each gear has a weight and this is reflected in how it interacts with the hand. The smaller lighter gears are easy to flick around with your hand. The larger heavier gears are not so easy and will swing your hand as a heavy weight will do in real life. This is an intentional behaviour to try and make the environment feel more physical and real. However, it can be disabled in the control panel by unchecking "use weighted gears".

Hopefully that helps, but otherwise respond to the email I sent and we'll work through it. 

David.

reviews.review_title: A new type of puzzle solving game.
reviews.review_description: This is a very interesting “puzzle” type of game. Let’s be clear that the player (you) will have many opportunities to have the crap scared out of you. Nice and creepy. In short, you must first locate the required gears that need to be placed on the gear-wall, and in the correct order to move to the next level. Cool thing is that the puzzles and time requirements to advance are very reasonable. You pickup and place the gears using  Psionic-type method. Highly recommend this game.

developer_response.body: I'm really glad you are enjoying Gear Out and thank you so much for leaving such a kind review. :-))

reviews.review_description:
Really like this game. Very different and the graphics a great. But I cannot get past level 13. The game glitches out. I had the gears all set and the game went completely black. Had to start the level over. Then finally did get through the door to the 3 chests of gold with the metal elevator but the elevator will not go up. The buttons do not work. So I’ll have to start level 13 yet again. I would love to get to the metal levels. So frustrated. Can anyone help? 
UPDATE..
Tech support has already reached out to me and is fixing the problems. Very quick response. Updated my review to 5 stars. Great game and great support. Thank you!

developer_response.body:
Hello. Thank you very much for the kind comments and for providing accurate feedback via the contact form at forthinteract.com. This is the best way of seeking support, as we don't get notifications that a review has been left, so could miss support requests here.

I've fixed the issue you discovered, which was that those nasty mummies on Level 13 could inadvertently lock the player in a room with no chance to escape! This has been fixed from version 2.06.

Thanks again for the kind comments and I hope you continue to enjoy Gear Out.

reviews.review_description: A little janky, but it's very good. I didn't get very far as I'm not smart enough for this sort of game - too hard for me. If you like puzzle games definitely try this.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for the great review :-) If you see anything janky, ley me know via the contact form at forthinteract.com and I'll look into it straight away.

reviews.review_title: perfect game if you like gears!
reviews.review_description: my 2nd favorite single player escape room

Oh WOW. Completely different to the usual escape room games that I recently got into but still immensely fun. Puzzles in the beginning of the levels are deceivingly easy but do get more and more challenging as you go on. I do not like  spider's on the best of days and this game does have a ton of spiders much to my dismay. Anyway, despite my phobia tendencies, it is still a simple but challenging game and a breath of fresh air from the other escape room challenges. The best thing about the game is that you can randomize puzzles and have endless challenges so it does not get stale after time. Furthermore the only addition that would make this game even greater than it already is is if it had a level editor but even without it I would highly recommend this game if you are into puzzles.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for such an encouraging review. I'm really glad you are enjoying Gear Out. A level editor is an interesting idea :-)

reviews.review_title: Dm for 1/4 the price. Great puzzle game!
reviews.review_description: I love game the puzzles are new for me haven't seen anything similar to this.

This game has 13 levels. All levels are about search for gears and arranging them, each level has different scenarios such as watery cave, crushing walls, rising water, and flying gears. There is an option to choose different numbers to create new puzzles which provides more variety. 

I'm currently at level 7 and I have fun so far.

developer_response.body: I'm glad you are enjoying it. Level 8 is quite scary :-) I'm currently working on the next 13 levels which will be a free update to the game.

reviews.review_description: A puzzle game with a difference, visually good especially for an app lab game, search for gears, solve the puzzle and move on to another room, can be a bit creepy but I would say not a true horror, perhaps the odd jump scare. Only played for 30 mins or so but thoroughly enjoying it. Looking forward to seeing where this goes and as to whether it leads on to a whole new area to explore.  The game has plenty of potential and I hope the devs keep moving forward with it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave such a detailed and encouraging review. There are currently 26 rooms, divided into Stone Age and Metal Age scenes. Each puzzle can be randomised up to 9,999 variations. If Gear Out is popular, I'll add a Sci Fi level too! Thanks again.

reviews.review_title: Wow creepy and a Brain work out.
reviews.review_description: This game was really fun after I changed the controls. I was able to move around easily. This game really immerses you with the dark caves and then causes you to think about how to find the gears and use them.￼

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for taking the time to post your experiences. Glad you are enjoying it. More brain-teasing levels coming soon. In the next set, the gear puzzles are double sided!

reviews.review_description:
In Gear Out you are trapped in a series of caves. You must explore the caves and find gears. You then need to figure out the correct order to place the gears on the wall to unlock the door to the next level.

There are a total of 13 levels to master, each one getting harder to finish then the last.

The levels are each unique with their own obstacles to over come. There are even a few fun jump scares you will come across while you explore. Finding the correct gears will take some good exploring and searching ever corner of the level. Then there is the challenge of putting the gears in the proper order to release the door. This starts off easy and ramps up in difficulty quick. I got stuck on level 5.

The game is fun to play and race the clock. The levels can be randomized, to keep things challenging and give it replay value. This is a fun puzzle game and is much more of a challenge then it looks. If you like puzzle games, this is a great one to play.   

If I could change one thing with the game, it would be to add strafing left to right. I found it a little hard to move around without it.

Watch my preview here,
https://youtu.be/Ys7NhHwsx2E

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the detailed review and especially for featuring Gear Out on your brilliant YouTube channel. Strafe Left/Right has been added now :-)

reviews.review_description: Puzzle game using gears, feel of escape rooms. A bit creepy, with a couple jump scares. A lot of fun. Not finished yet, but enjoying it. Recommended if you like puzzle games.
A great game with great graphics and easy to use controls. A different approach to the ordinary escape room though, making it all the more exciting

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your encouraging feedback. Glad you are enjoying Gear Out. :-)

reviews.review_title: Great game at a great price!
reviews.review_description:
I have just recently purchased this game and it is quite fun and can be very challenging. Graphics are really good as well. I did have a glitch in the game that would not allow me to continue playing but
after being in contact with the developers within an afternoon of posting my issues with the game they were very quick to come up with a fix that will allow me to continue playing, now that’s great customer service, thank you so much!!   As for anyone that is interested in some challenging gear puzzles then this is definitely a game you’ll enjoy at a very good price!!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the 5* review and I'm glad you're enjoying Gear Out. Especially thank you for sending the bug report via the contact form at forthinteract.com with video recording which allowed me to track it down and fix it quickly. Best of luck playing the remaining levels!

reviews.review_description: Gears isn't your typical puzzle game - it's a whole experience. If you're into challenging puzzles, eerie vibes, a touch of steampunk, and the unexpected this game will keep you entertained for hours. Please keep adding more levels. 5 Stars Recommended.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for you kind review. I've tried to make Gear Out as immersive as possible so I'm glad that's working out!

reviews.review_description:
Stuck it on this morning 
Amazing graphics for app lab game, Devs did an excellent job creating an eerie environment, my brain will probably explode but love a challenge.
Thanks guys The  Room  VR has some competition.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for such an encouraging review. Hope your brain doesn't explode :-)

reviews.review_title: A VR Escape Room Game You Should Try
reviews.review_description:
Gear Out is a great destination for VR enthusiasts and escape room lovers. The game transported me to a mysterious location, where I had to find and collect different gears hidden in various places. Some of the gears were easy to spot, while others required me to solve puzzles, manipulate objects, and explore the environment.

There are plenty of levels to progress through, which added to the challenge and excitement. The game was very rewarding and satisfying, and I felt a rush of adrenaline every time I escaped a room. It's definitely a game you shouldn't miss out on.
Arrange gears ⚙️ on the wall. Some are timed and each puzzle is different and has a different scenario. Love the game and am only half way through it
this game is legit, fun, could use some movement fixes but im excited for the end game, maybe for fun add some creepy undertones or ghosts to pressure you to finish the puzzle faster.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the positive feedback. Feel free to contact me about possible improvements for movement (forwardthinkinginteractive.com/gear-out). There are many movement options already, but they are in the game's control panel. To make it easier for first-time players, I've added a controller pop up so users can select from the most common set ups from the start. I like the idea about ghosts - there are skeletons and mummies at the moment on the later levels.
